text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He knew the secrets of the forest better than anyone.

One sunny afternoon, Oliver watched as a group of children from the village played near his favorite perch. They liked to throw breadcrumbs into the woods. Oliver had a habit of finding them and hid them somewhere safe for them to find later.

The children ran back home, laughing, their faces sprinkled with crumbs. Oliver hooted contentedly, grateful for their joy. Suddenly, a loud noise shattered the peace.

From the other side of the forest came a bear, growling menacingly. He stomped with all his might right towards the children's path.

Just in time, Oliver swooped down from his high branch, clucking loudly. The bear stopped short, looking at the giant creature before him rather than the children. Slowly, with no real fear, the bear sniffed Oliver's feathers. He seemed curious
